executive_summary: The Iran-US armed conflict has escalated significantly with confirmed Iranian strikes on a UK military base in Cyprus and attacks on Western oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. The death of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei has triggered coordinated proxy retaliation from Hezbollah (rockets on Haifa) while Israeli forces intensify operations across Beirut. The 24h incident count has risen to 770, with the conflict now spanning six distinct geographic fronts.
whats_new: UK MOD confirms Iranian drone strike on RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus at midnight local time - Iranian capabilities now extending into Eastern Mediterranean
IRGC claims strikes on 3 US/British oil tankers attempting to exit Persian Gulf through Strait of Hormuz - targeting Western energy infrastructure
UK PM Starmer confirms US can use British bases for defensive missile strikes on Iran - direct UK involvement in conflict
Trump warns 'likely more' US troop deaths before conflict ends, acknowledging 3 killed and 5 seriously wounded in Kuwait strike
Iranian projectiles fired from Lebanon toward Israeli territory - expanding northern front
Hezbollah explicitly links Haifa rocket attack to Khamenei assassination, claiming revenge for 'blood of Imam'
confirmed_events: Iranian ballistic missiles targeted Israeli Cabinet Committee meeting in Jerusalem during session - direct attack on Israeli leadership
Bahrain confirms downing 61 missiles and 34 Iranian drones at Salman Port and Fifth Fleet base - major interception effort
Israeli Defense Forces announce strikes on Hezbollah command centers across Beirut and southern Lebanon - intensified northern campaign
Hezbollah announces targeting Israeli missile defense site in Haifa with rockets and drones - strategic degradation of Israeli air defense
Multiple explosions confirmed in Tehran and Karaj as part of ongoing US-Israeli strike campaign on Iranian territory
unverified_emerging: Unverified citizen reports claim US fighter jets bombed Tabriz - potential major escalation requiring corroboration (source: citizen reports)
Approximately 170 students and teachers killed in US-Israel attacks on Iran - casualty figure needs verification (source: RIA Novosti)
Reports of Iranian Shahed-136 drone flying openly over Dubai - potential intelligence/security concern if confirmed (source: Press TV)

people_travel: All commercial flights to/from Gulf states severely disrupted due to missile activity and airspace closures
UK advises against all travel to Israel, Lebanon, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar
RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus operational but under active threat - military mobility impacted
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz under active threat with IRGC tanker strikes - global oil supply chain at severe risk
Salman Port in Bahrain targeted - major Gulf logistics hub disrupted
Gulf shipping routes facing unprecedented threat levels with multiple commercial vessels targeted
market_macro: Global energy markets bracing for shock as Hormuz closure threatens - oil prices spiking
Saudi Arabia summoned Iran envoy amid escalating Gulf crisis - regional economic partnerships fracturing
Qatar and Kuwait protest Iranian attacks - US-aligned Gulf states consolidating against Tehran

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict will intensify over the next 24-72h with Iran likely executing additional strikes on Gulf shipping and US regional bases. Khamenei's death creates leadership vacuum but IRGC remains operationally capable - expect continued missile barrages toward Israel and US assets. Hezbollah has committed to sustained operations, signaling the northern front will escalate. UK involvement via Cyprus base strikes now makes this a multi-national conflict.
escalation_risks: Strait of Hormuz closure could trigger global energy crisis and international economic collapse
Iranian drone strike on Cyprus base may prompt UK direct retaliation - expanding conflict to NATO territory
Hezbollah targeting Israeli air defense systems could degrade Israel's Iron Dome and invite larger Iranian missile strikes
Regional spillover: 10 killed in Karachi protests at US consulate - conflict spreading to South Asia
Syria and Iraq proxy fronts may activate, adding additional US military exposure
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears unlikely in near-term given Trump administration stance that 'more US deaths expected' and IRGC framing of attacks as defensive retaliation. Only pathway would be complete IRGC military infrastructure destruction or decisive regime change - both requiring sustained major combat operations. International mediation (UN, Russia, China) has not emerged as factor despite global economic stakes.
